title: Bayesian analysis with Stan
text: Posterior medians 95% HPD intervals (exponentiated).
code: td_b <- tidy(fit, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) hpd <- credint(fit, prob = 0.95, type = "HPD") td_b$hpd.low <- exp(hpd[, 1]) td_b$hpd.high <- exp(hpd[, 2]) td_b$term <- factor(td_b$term, levels = rev(td_b$term))  ggplot(td_b, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +   geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = hpd.low, xmax = hpd.high), linewidth = 0.4, color = "steelblue") +   labs(x = "Hazard ratio (posterior median)", y = NULL, title = "95% HPD intervals") +   theme_bw()

title: Bayesian analysis with Stan
text: MLE 95% CI vs Bayesian 95% HPD (exponentiated).
code: td_m <- tidy(fit_mle, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) cmp <- merge(   td_m[, c("term", "estimate", "conf.low", "conf.high")],   td_b[, c("term", "estimate", "hpd.low", "hpd.high")],   by = "term",   suffixes = c("_mle", "_bayes") ) cmp_long <- rbind(   data.frame(term = cmp$term, method = "MLE", low = cmp$conf.low, high = cmp$conf.high,              est = cmp$estimate_mle),   data.frame(term = cmp$term, method = "Bayes", low = cmp$hpd.low, high = cmp$hpd.high,              est = cmp$estimate_bayes) ) cmp_long$term <- factor(cmp_long$term, levels = rev(unique(cmp$term)))  ggplot(cmp_long, aes(x = est, y = term, color = method)) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +   geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = low, xmax = high), position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), linewidth = 0.4) +   labs(x = "Hazard ratio", y = NULL, color = NULL) +   theme_bw()

title: Bernstein polynomial degree and baseline plots
text: late events → prefer moderate degree; inspect baseline smoothness.

title: Bernstein polynomial degree and baseline plots
code: fit_low <- bpph(   Surv(time, status) ~ karno,   degree = 3,   data = veteran,   approach = "mle",   init = 0 ) fit_high <- bpph(   Surv(time, status) ~ karno,   degree = 8,   data = veteran,   approach = "mle",   init = 0 ) c(low = length(fit_low$bp.param), high = length(fit_high$bp.param)) #>  low high  #>    3    8 AIC(fit_low, fit_high) #>      fit model degree      aic npars #> 1 model1    ph      3 1458.842     4 #> 2 model2    ph      8 1465.575     9

title: Bernstein polynomial degree and baseline plots
text: Cumulative baseline hazard: degree 3 vs degree 8. Estimated baseline hazard components (degree 8). Bernstein basis functions [0, tau].
code: times <- seq(0, max(veteran$time), length.out = 100) tau <- max(veteran$time)  baseline_curve <- function(fit, times, tau) {   bb <- bp.basis(times, degree = length(fit$bp.param), tau = tau)   data.frame(     time = times,     cumhaz = as.vector(bb$G %*% fit$bp.param),     degree = paste0("m = ", length(fit$bp.param))   ) }  bl_df <- rbind(   baseline_curve(fit_low, times, tau),   baseline_curve(fit_high, times, tau) )  ggplot(bl_df, aes(x = time, y = cumhaz, color = degree)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Cumulative baseline hazard", color = NULL) +   theme_bw() plot(fit_high, graph = "baseline", cumulative = FALSE) plot(fit_high, graph = "basis", cumulative = FALSE)

title: Bernstein polynomial degree and baseline plots

title: Bernstein polynomial degree and baseline plots

title: Model checking and residuals
text: Martingale residuals scatter around zero without strong trend fitted cumulative hazard. Martingale residuals vs fitted cumulative hazard (Cox-Snell). Martingale residuals cell type.
code: mart <- residuals(fit, type = "martingale") csnell <- residuals(fit, type = "cox-snell") fitted_ch <- csnell mart_df <- data.frame(   fitted = fitted_ch,   martingale = mart,   status = factor(veteran$status),   celltype = veteran$celltype ) ggplot(mart_df, aes(x = fitted, y = martingale, color = status)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +   geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-4, 2)) +   labs(     x = "Fitted cumulative hazard (Cox-Snell)",     y = "Martingale residual",     color = "Event"   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") ggplot(mart_df, aes(x = fitted, y = martingale)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +   facet_wrap(~ celltype, scales = "free_x") +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-4, 2)) +   labs(x = "Fitted cumulative hazard", y = "Martingale residual") +   theme_bw()

title: Model checking and residuals
text: systematic trend LOESS smooth (e.g. upward curve high fitted values) suggests covariate baseline misspecification. Faceting reveals subgroups model fits poorly.

title: Model checking and residuals
text: well-specified model, Kaplan-Meier exp(-CS) track Exponential(1) reference (45-degree line cumulative-hazard scale). Cox-Snell calibration: KM exp(-CS) vs Exponential(1) reference.
code: km_cs <- survfit(Surv(csnell, veteran$status) ~ 1) cs_df <- data.frame(   cs = km_cs$time,   cumhaz = -log(pmax(km_cs$surv, .Machine$double.eps)) ) ggplot(cs_df, aes(x = cs, y = cumhaz)) +   geom_step(linewidth = 0.6) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "steelblue", linetype = "dashed") +   labs(     x = "Cox-Snell residual",     y = "Nelson-Aalen cumulative hazard of exp(-CS)",     title = "Cox-Snell calibration"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Model checking and residuals
text: Large deviance residuals flag observations poorly explained fitted model. Deviance residuals (largest magnitudes highlighted).
code: dev <- residuals(fit, type = "deviance") dev_df <- data.frame(   index = seq_along(dev),   deviance = dev,   highlight = abs(dev) >= quantile(abs(dev), 0.95) ) ggplot(dev_df, aes(x = index, y = deviance, color = highlight)) +   geom_point(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.7) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey40", "TRUE" = "firebrick"), guide = "none") +   labs(x = "Observation index", y = "Deviance residual") +   theme_bw()

title: Model checking and residuals
text: extreme points → investigate subjects; consider robustness conclusions. Many extremes → global lack fit; revisit model structure.

title: Model checking and residuals
text: spsurv vs Cox martingale residuals. Disagreement expected general (different likelihoods baselines). Large outliers comparison warrant case-level review.
code: cox_fit <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ karno + celltype, data = veteran) mart_cox <- residuals(cox_fit, type = "martingale") ggplot(data.frame(bp = mart, cox = mart_cox), aes(x = bp, y = cox)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.8) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +   labs(x = "spsurv martingale", y = "Cox martingale") +   theme_bw()

title: Getting started with spsurv
text: Kaplan-Meier survival larynx cancer stage.
code: library(spsurv) library(KMsurv) library(survival) library(ggplot2) library(generics) data(larynx) larynx$stage <- factor(larynx$stage) censor_tbl <- do.call(rbind, lapply(split(larynx, larynx$stage), function(d) {   data.frame(     stage = as.character(d$stage[1]),     n = nrow(d),     events = sum(d$delta),     censored = sum(1 - d$delta),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) })) censor_tbl$pct_censored <- round(100 * censor_tbl$censored / censor_tbl$n, 1) censor_tbl #>   stage  n events censored pct_censored #> 1     1 33     15       18         54.5 #> 2     2 17      7       10         58.8 #> 3     3 27     17       10         37.0 #> 4     4 13     11        2         15.4 km_stage <- survfit(Surv(time, delta) ~ stage, data = larynx) km_long <- data.frame(   time = km_stage$time,   surv = km_stage$surv,   stage = rep(levels(larynx$stage), km_stage$strata) ) ggplot(km_long, aes(x = time, y = surv, color = stage)) +   geom_step(linewidth = 0.6) +   labs(x = "Time (years)", y = "Survival probability", color = "Stage") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Choosing PH, PO, and AFT models
text: Kaplan-Meier cell type. Karnofsky score event status. paper examples sometimes restrict prior == 0; use full veteran dataset simplicity.
code: library(spsurv) library(survival) library(ggplot2) data(veteran) veteran$celltype <- factor(veteran$celltype) km_ct <- survfit(Surv(time, status) ~ celltype, data = veteran) km_long <- data.frame(   time = km_ct$time,   surv = km_ct$surv,   celltype = rep(levels(veteran$celltype), km_ct$strata) ) ggplot(km_long, aes(x = time, y = surv, color = celltype)) +   geom_step(linewidth = 0.6) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Survival probability", color = "Cell type") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) ggplot(veteran, aes(x = factor(status), y = karno, fill = factor(status))) +   geom_violin(alpha = 0.6) +   geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.alpha = 0.4) +   scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Censored", "Event")) +   labs(x = NULL, y = "Karnofsky score", fill = NULL) +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Choosing PH, PO, and AFT models
text: AFT uses degree = 3 Bernstein gamma block stays numerically stable veteran; PH PO use degree = 5. overlay plots point estimates survival; gamma_information_stable FALSE, bands still computed survfit() warns may unreliable.

code: newdata <- data.frame(karno = c(30, 70), celltype = "squamous") plot_times <- seq(0, max(veteran$time), length.out = 121)  pr_ph <- predict(fit_ph, newdata = newdata, times = plot_times) pr_po <- predict(fit_po, newdata = newdata, times = plot_times) pr_aft <- predict(fit_aft, newdata = newdata, times = plot_times)  pr_ph$family <- "PH" pr_po$family <- "PO" pr_aft$family <- "AFT" pr_all <- rbind(pr_ph, pr_po, pr_aft) pr_all$label <- paste0(   pr_all$family, " (Karnofsky ",   newdata$karno[match(as.character(pr_all$id), as.character(seq_len(nrow(newdata))))],   ")" )  ggplot(pr_all, aes(x = time, y = surv, color = label, linetype = family)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Survival probability", color = NULL, linetype = "Family") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") aic_tbl <- data.frame(   model = c("PH", "PO", "AFT"),   AIC = c(AIC(fit_ph), AIC(fit_po), AIC(fit_aft)) ) aic_tbl #>   model      AIC #> 1    PH 1450.026 #> 2    PO 1438.685 #> 3   AFT 1464.954

title: Bernstein Polynomial Based Semiparametric Survival Analysis
text: spsurv: R package semi-parametric survival analysis. spsurv package designed contribute flexible set semi-parametric survival regression options, including proportional hazards (PH), proportional odds (PO), accelerated failure time (AFT) models right-censored data. package provides: Survival classes (PH, PO, AFT) extensions based fully likelihood-based approach either Bayesian maximum likelihood (ML) estimation procedures Smooth estimates unknown baseline functions based Bernstein polynomial (BP) Integration Stan user-defined modeling Six distinct prior specification options Bayesian analysis Stan open-source platform language log-probability functions custom likelihoods priors. Access Stan R provided via rstan; package uses NUTS (-U-Turn) sampling default Bayesian fits.

title: Bernstein Polynomial Based Semiparametric Survival Analysis
code: install.packages("spsurv")

title: Bernstein Polynomial Based Semiparametric Survival Analysis
code: install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("rvpanaro/spsurv")

title: Bernstein Polynomial Based Semiparametric Survival Analysis
text: Fit BP-based survival regression PH model. degree omitted, default ceiling(sqrt(n)) n number rows data; resolved value stored fit fit$degree. Alternatively, use spbp function set degree bernstein(m): Bayesian analysis approach argument: Nested model comparison (MLE) follows anova() patterns survival::survreg: See reference manual vignette vignette("getting-started", package = "spsurv") examples.
code: library("KMsurv") data("larynx") larynx$stage <- factor(larynx$stage)  library(spsurv)  fit <- bpph(Surv(time, delta) ~ age + stage, data = larynx, approach = "mle") summary(fit) fit$degree   # Bernstein polynomial degree used fit <- spbp(Surv(time, delta) ~ age + stage, model = "ph", data = larynx,             dist = bernstein(5), approach = "mle") summary(fit) fit2 <- spbp(Surv(time, delta) ~ age + stage,              approach = "bayes", data = larynx,              iter = 2000, chains = 1, warmup = 1000) summary(fit2) fit0 <- bpph(Surv(time, delta) ~ 1, data = larynx, approach = "mle", degree = 5) fit1 <- bpph(Surv(time, delta) ~ age, data = larynx, approach = "mle", degree = 5) fit2 <- bpph(Surv(time, delta) ~ age + stage, data = larynx, approach = "mle", degree = 5)  anova(fit0, fit1) anova(fit1, fit2) anova(fit2)   # sequential term-wise table

title: Bernstein Polynomial Based Semiparametric Survival Analysis
text: Coefficient model-level summaries follow generics / broom convention: publication-ready tables, pipe tidy() output gt similar packages.
code: library(generics) library(ggplot2)  fit <- bpph(Surv(time, delta) ~ age + stage, data = larynx, approach = "mle")  # One row per term (hazard ratios with 95% CI) td <- tidy(fit, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) print(td)  # One-row model summary (n, log-likelihood, global LR test, AIC, ...) glance(fit)  # Forest plot ggplot(td, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +   geom_point() +   geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +   labs(x = "Hazard ratio", y = NULL)

what: Baseline inference, diagnostics, and model ranking
title: Bernstein Polynomial Based Semiparametric Survival Analysis
text: also use survstan package, qualify spsurv helpers library(survstan) — e.g. spsurv::estimates(fit) spsurv::se(fit).
code: # Baseline gamma weights with Wald table (survstan-style summary) summary(fit, show_baseline = TRUE) print(fit, show_baseline = TRUE)   # same via print() print(fit, bp.param = TRUE)        # baseline-only table  # Tidy baseline weights tidy(fit, component = "baseline", conf.int = TRUE) confint(fit, parm = names(fit$bp.param))  # Rank nested models by AIC f0 <- bpph(Surv(time, delta) ~ 1, data = larynx, degree = 5) f1 <- bpph(Surv(time, delta) ~ age, data = larynx, degree = 5) rank_models(f0, f1)                # or AIC(f0, f1)  # Baseline survival S0(t) at event times survfit(fit, baseline = TRUE, tidy = TRUE)  # ggplot2 residual plots (requires ggplot2) ggresiduals(fit, type = "martingale")

code: library(parsnip) library(censored) library(workflows) library(spsurv)  wf <- workflow() |>   add_formula(Surv(time, status) ~ karno) |>   add_model(     proportional_hazards() |>       set_engine("spsurv", degree = 5, scale = FALSE, init = 0)   )  fit_wf <- fit(wf, data = veteran) predict(fit_wf, veteran[1:2, ], type = "survival", eval_time = 100)

text: Bayesian fits, always check: divergent transitions (target: 0) split-(R) (target: close 1, typically < 1.01) effective sample sizes (avoid low bulk/tail ESS) warnings appear, use escalation order: increase adapt_delta (example, 0.8 0.9 0.95) increase iter warmup re-check divergences, Rhat, ESS interpretation Higher adapt_delta usually reduces divergences increases runtime, tuning balance stability compute cost. Please report issues https://github.com/rvpanaro/spsurv/issues contact maintainer (see DESCRIPTION).

title: The 'spsurv' package. — spsurv-package
text: spbp fits semi-parametric models time--event survival data. Non-informative right-censoring assumption available. user-defined Bernstein polynomial can user-defined using arbitrary degree, .e. highest basis polynomials order. framework takes advantage fully likelihood methods since polynomial parameters used estimate baseline functions. Even , said semi-parametric since approach rely distribution. Unlike Cox model, BP based models provide smooth hazard survival curve estimates. Bayesian fits, users routinely inspect divergences, split-\(\hat R\), effective sample sizes, tighten NUTS controls (e.g., increasing adapt_delta, iter, warmup) warnings present.

title: spsurv 1.1.0
text: CRAN resubmission: Maintainer contact updated rvpanaro@gmail.com; Authors@R roles reviewed (single maintainer; thesis advisors listed ths, rev code review feedback); package Description citation updated <doi:10.48550/arXiv.2003.10548>; set Config/rstantools/auto_config: FALSE ship committed Stan C++ exports (install-time configure/rstan_config(), avoiding Windows R-devel rstan.dll load failures); added RcppParallel synced src/Makevars.win TBB flags Unix Makevars; restored covr, rsample, rsurv Suggests optional tests referenced tests/. Inference robustness: .spbp_gamma_information_diagnostics() longer calls qr() non-finite gamma information matrices; vcov()/survfit() return NA uncertainty warning block unavailable. tidymodels: parsnip engines spsurv (MLE) spsurv_bayes proportional_hazards(), new proportional_odds(), survival_reg(); bp_survival_reg() convenience constructor; workflow / predict(type = "survival") support. tidybayes: as_draws_df.spbp(), spread_surv_draws.spbp(), S3 hooks tidy_draws / spread_draws / gather_draws Bayes fits; posterior draw indices (.chain, .iteration, .draw). Prediction: censored-style predict.spbp() types (survival, time, linear_pred); augment.spbp(). Vignette: vignette("tidymodels"). Inference: vcov.spbp() longer applies diagonal ridge (polish removed); beta standard errors unchanged Bernstein block stable longer inflated ill-conditioned. Summary: summary.spbp() now includes coef_interval component (Wald MLE, HPD Bayes) prints show_intervals = TRUE. Repository: paper workflow moved inst/ paper/ (excluded package tarball). Dependencies: trimmed Suggests packages used vignettes, tests, optional integrations; dev tools (devtools, roxygen2, covr) unused tidymodels/rsurv entries removed (install transitively needed locally).

title: spsurv 1.0.2
text: Vignettes: Expanded EDA plots, visual checks, decision guidance across seven articles. tidy/broom: tidy(), glance(), logLik(), AIC(), anova(), estimates(), se() methods spbp objects; bernstein() baseline specification. Prediction: predict() improved survfit() (tidy output, newdata covariates, Bayesian monotone bands). Residuals: residuals() supports martingale, deviance, Cox-Snell types; martingale diagnostics fixed examples. Handlers: Removed handlers.R; validation setup logic inlined spbp.default. vcov: Block-wise inversion Hessian (regression vs Bernstein polynomial blocks) vcov.spbp. Stan: Slim transformed parameters (alpha stored); pointwise log_lik moved generated quantities; likelihood via bp_pointwise_log_lik() model block. Stan (AFT): clamping u avoid boundary bias; minimum feasible divisor (min_range) range degenerate. MLE: Initial values optimizer drawn prior distributions; retries limited 3 attempts. spbp objects: Component degree fits call$degree; default Bernstein degree ceiling(sqrt(n)) omitted (documented help, README, vignettes). anova.spbp: Pairwise sequential analysis--deviance tables aligned survival::survreg (Terms, Test, Df, Deviance, etc.). bpph / bppo / bpaft: User-facing match.call() without unevaluated dist/baseline symbols; optional degree argument. Documentation: Expanded spbp / spbp.default help (@details, return components, vignette links); README usage model-comparison examples updated. Examples: data("veteran", package = "survival") docs examples. pkgdown: Reference index updated match current package; Bootstrap 5 / default template; logo size alt-text; README aligned site style. Repository: CRAN submission hygiene (.gitignore, .Rbuildignore, LICENSE).
